     The Numbers Business Report

Carnage (2011)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $2,546,747Details
International Box Office $35,565,407
Worldwide Box Office $38,112,154
Further financial details...

Synopsis

An ensemble comedy, centered on parental differences. After two boys duke it out on a playground, the parents of the “victim” invite the parents of the “bully” over to work out their issues. A polite discussion of childrearing soon escalates into verbal warfare, with all four parents revealing their true colors. None of them will escape the carnage.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$79,795 (3.1% of total gross)
Legs:3.58 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:6.7% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$25,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.5 times production budget)
Theater counts:5 opening theaters/494 max. theaters, 2.2 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $3,828,492

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: December 16th, 2011 (Limited) by Sony Pictures Classics
International Releases: December 30th, 2011 (Wide), released as O Deus da Carnificina (Portugal)
February 18th, 2012 (Wide) (Japan)
March 1st, 2012 (Wide) (Serbia and Montenegro)
March 8th, 2012 (Wide) (Argentina)
March 23rd, 2012 (Wide) (South Africa)
Video Release: March 20th, 2012 by Sony Pictures Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for language.
(Rating bulletin 2191, 9/28/2011)
Running Time: 79 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: One Location, One Set
Plot point: Dysfunctional Family, Lawyers
Source material: Novel or Other Work Adapted by Author
Subgenre: Black Comedy
Source:Based on Play
Genre:Black Comedy
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Said Ben Said, SBS Productions, Constantin Film, SPI Film Studio, Versatil Cinema, Zanagar Films, France 2 Cinema, Canal Plus, Cinecinema, France Television, Centropolis Entertainment, Polish Film Institute, ARD/Degeto, Filmfermsehfonds Bayem, Wild Bunch
Production Countries: France, Germany, Poland, Spain
Languages: English

Synopsis

An ensemble comedy, centered on parental differences. After two boys duke it out on a playground, the parents of the “victim” invite the parents of the “bully” over to work out their issues. A polite discussion of childrearing soon escalates into verbal warfare, with all four parents revealing their true colors. None of them will escape the carnage.
